domain: metallurgy / smithing (pattern-welding: forge-weld alternating high/low-carbon steel layers via fold-and-hammer cycles)
specific_mechanism: Pattern welding forge-welds two or more steel compositions (often high-C and low-C) in alternating layers; folding multiplies the layers exponentially (each fold doubles count). Bonds form below fusion via interatomic contact under hammer pressure, with flux removing oxide contamination. Modern research shows carbon DIFFUSES across the layer boundaries so 'hard' and 'soft' layers homogenize — the visual pattern is a residue, not a hardness map. The mechanical property is the SHEAR resistance of the layer interfaces.
llm_application: Pattern-welded adapter stacking: forge-weld alternating LoRA adapters trained on complementary corpora (high-density code adapter ↔ low-density chat adapter) by fold-and-hammer fine-tuning cycles. Each cycle folds adapter weights with a small noise injection and re-runs short SFT; the iterative folding multiplies effective layer count and homogenizes capability across the model. Basin-stability: the homogenized-but-layered structure has a wider performance basin under prompt-perturbation than either pure adapter.
novelty_claim: LoRAFusion (2510.00206), CompAs compositional adapters, FedAMoLE heterogeneous federated personalization, block-pool dynamic assembly (USENIX ATC25) — none specifically frames adapter composition as pattern-welded fold-and-hammer cycles where iterative folding multiplies effective layer count and shear-resistance of the interface is the performance proxy.
